title: Tree Skeleton Recognition
description:
A binary tree's skeleton is described by its shape. Given a level-order array (using 'N' for absent nodes), determine: is it a perfect binary tree, a complete binary tree, or just a regular binary tree?
- Perfect: all leaves at the same depth, every internal node has 2 children.
- Complete: all levels full except possibly last, which is filled left-to-right.
- Other: none of the above.
inputSpec: A single line: the level-order encoding. 'N' means null. Example: 1 2 3 4 5 6 7

botCodes.code:
vals=input().split()
n=len(vals)
# Check perfect: n = 2^k - 1 for some k
import math
h=math.log2(n+1)
def is_int(x):return abs(x-round(x))<1e-9
if is_int(h) and all(v!='N' for v in vals):
    print('PERFECT')
else:
    # Complete: no N before a non-N
    found_n=False;ok=True
    for v in vals:
        if v=='N':found_n=True
        elif found_n:ok=False;break
    print('COMPLETE' if ok else 'OTHER')
